title: Please consider "none" logdriver for containers started by `podman run`
body:
### Feature request description

It seems to me the usecase for `podman run` is to run a one-off container and immediately provide output. Possibly even provide an interactive experience with `-i`.

Especially when paired with `--rm`, for such containers it seems pointless to store logs.


### Suggest potential solution

For `podman run`, default `--log-driver` to `none`.

For `podman start`, keep the existing default.


### Have you considered any alternatives?

Obviously I can pass `--log-driver none` to all `podman run` invocations, but as `run` already seems to be a convenience interface, it could also take care of this.

### Additional context

Just so we are on the same page, `podman run` sends stdout to the terminal it is run from:

```shell
$ podman run docker.io/library/debian:trixie-slim /bin/echo HIHIHI
HIHIHI
```

(This also works for podman-remote/machine setups like macOS.)

comments.body:
There is a fair amount of prior discussion about this in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/14743. I suggest you "revive" that thread there if you have new arguments. For now you can set the driver in the containers.conf config file if you really know you don't want logs.

I close this to keep the conversation in place
okay, i see. I'll just make a wrapper.
